title: Thelypteris seemannii
taxonomy: Thelypteris seemannii is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. The family Thelypteridaceae is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce by spores. Within the family Thelypteridaceae, Thelypteris seemannii is often placed in the subfamily Thelypteridoideae. The classification of Thelypteris seemannii is based on morphological and molecular studies, which have helped to clarify its relationships within the plant kingdom.
uses: Thelypteris seemannii, a species of fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and roots are used to treat different ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
